entries.title: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
entries.snippet: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.

entries.title: Trump's rift with Pope is playing out in public - it's costing him valuable support
entries.snippet: Leading conservative Catholics tell the BBC why they back the American pontiff in his spat with Trump.

entries.title: Frank Gardner: What is China's role in the Iran war?
entries.snippet: BBC Security Correspondent Frank Gardner explains how the world's second-largest economy fits into the Gulf conflict.

entries.title: Trump threatens to fire Fed chair Powell if he doesn't leave in May
entries.snippet: Donald Trump's threat marks the latest escalation in his ongoing spat with Jerome Powell.

entries.title: Nine killed in second Turkish school shooting in two days
entries.snippet: Eight students and one teacher died in the attack, according to Interior Minister Mustafa Cifci.

entries.title: Prominent Palestinian prisoner Marwan Barghouti assaulted three times in a month, family says
entries.snippet: The Israeli Prison Service says the allegation that Barghouti was subjected to physical violence by prison guards are "false and baseless".

entries.title: Israeli triple-tap strike kills three paramedics in Lebanon, officials say
entries.snippet: Lebanon's government condemns as a "flagrant crime" the killing of the paramedics, one of whom featured in a BBC report.

entries.title: Ticketmaster-owner Live Nation ran a monopoly and overcharged fans, jury finds
entries.snippet: The lawsuit said the firm's practices had led to higher ticket prices and worse service for customers.

entries.title: South Africa names apartheid-era politician as new ambassador to the US
entries.snippet: Roelf Meyer played a key role as a negotiator during talks to end white-minority rule in South Africa.

entries.title: Chinese national given one year in prison for smuggling ants out of Kenya
entries.snippet: Zhang Kequn was arrested in March while trying to travel to China with more than 2,000 live ants.

entries.title: SantaCon organiser charged with stealing $1m from charity pub crawl
entries.snippet: Prosecutors say Stefan Pildes spent hundreds of thousands of $2.7m raised for charity on personal expenses.

entries.title: South Korea jails American YouTuber for public nuisance
entries.snippet: Johnny Somali sparked outrage after kissing a statue representing World War Two sex slaves.

entries.title: Magyar calls for fast handover of power in talks with Hungary's president
entries.snippet: Péter Magyar, the man who ended Viktor Orbán's 16 years of continuous rule, wants the new parliament to convene in early May.

entries.title: Australia's richest person must share part of her mining fortunes, court rules
entries.snippet: The long-running legal dispute is over a lucrative iron ore project in the top west corner of Australia.

entries.title: Sri Lanka repatriates 238 Iranian sailors stranded after US torpedo attack
entries.snippet: The survivors of the US attack on Iranian vessel Iris Dena, which claimed the lives of 104 people, were among those sent home.

entries.title: 250 missing after migrant boat sinks in Indian Ocean
entries.snippet: The trawler "reportedly sank due to heavy winds, rough seas and overcrowding", the United Nations said.

entries.title: Kanye West postpones France gig following UK ban
entries.snippet: It comes a week after the UK government announced it would block the rapper from entering the country.

entries.title: Australian pleads guilty to creating deepfake porn in landmark case
entries.snippet: The 19-year-old is the first person to be charged under a new national law.

entries.title: 'Bit of pain' worth long-term security from Iran, Bessent tells BBC
entries.snippet: US Treasury Secretary said a "small bit of economic pain" was worth it to eliminate the threat of Iranian strikes on Western capitals.

entries.title: Starmer says he's 'not going to yield' to pressure from Trump on Iran war
entries.snippet: It follows a warning from the US president that America's trade deal with the UK "can always be changed".

entries.title: Middle East conflict causes a fluoride shortage for US drinking water
entries.snippet: Some U.S. water systems are cutting back on fluoride because of a key chemical is in short supply. Israel is one of its main producers.

entries.title: Popes have spoken out on politics before. But with Trump and Pope Leo it's different
entries.snippet: President Trump's attacks on Pope Leo are unprecedented, religious experts told NPR. Here's how the situation differs from other popes' political critiques.

entries.title: Visionary NPR leader Kevin Klose, who led network to new heights, has died
entries.snippet: Klose led NPR for a decade starting in 1998, a period of incredible growth for the public media network.

entries.title: Once again, Trump threatens to fire Fed Chair Jerome Powell
entries.snippet: President Trump once again threatened to fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and doubled down on a discredited probe of the central bank.

entries.title: Greetings from Nairobi, where taking a matatu is no ordinary bus ride
entries.snippet: The moment you board, the music grabs you. These privately owned, brightly painted minibuses are moving canvases, mobile sound systems — rolling declarations of what young Nairobi finds cool.

entries.title: Trump administration moves to erase Jan. 6 riot convictions for seditious conspiracy
entries.snippet: The Trump administration is moving to vacate the seditious conspiracy convictions of extremists involved in the Jan. 6, 2021, attack who earlier received commutations instead of full pardons.

entries.title: Trump says new talks with Iran will happen soon. And, Eric Swalwell faces new allegations
entries.snippet: President Trump says new talks with Iran could happen in the next two days. And, Democrat Eric Swalwell faces new allegations as a second woman comes forward accusing him of rape.

entries.title: Trump says Iran talks could resume in days as U.S. military blocks Iranian ports
entries.snippet: President Trump said a second round of direct U.S.-Iran peace talks could resume in Pakistan within the next two days, even as he instituted a naval blockade of all Iranian ports.

entries.title: Fela Kuti is the first African artist to enter the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
entries.snippet: In December, the late Nigerian superstar became the first African musician to get a Grammy lifetime achievement award. Now he's making history as well at the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame.

entries.title: Tax season was supposed to bring big refunds. So far they're less than expected
entries.snippet: The average refund so far is $350 more than last year at this time, despite projections that it would be closer to $1,000 due to Republican-led tax changes as part of the Big Beautiful Bill Act.

entries.title: Justice Jackson calls out Supreme Court conservatives' pro-Trump emergency orders
entries.snippet: Supreme Court Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson has delivered a sustained attack on her conservative colleagues' use of emergency orders to benefit the Trump administration, calling the orders "scratch-paper musings" that can "seem oblivious and thus ring hollow."

entries.title: FDA to weigh easing limits on unproven peptides favored by RFK Jr. and MAHA supporters
entries.snippet: The Food and Drug Administration will hold a meeting this summer to consider easing restrictions on more than a half dozen peptide injections, a group of unapproved therapies that have become popular among wellness influencers, fitness gurus and celebrities.

entries.title: Pakistani delegation meets in Tehran hoping for more U.S.-Iran talks before ceasefire expires
entries.snippet: Pakistan's army chief met Wednesday in Tehran with Iran's foreign minister in the latest diplomatic move to ease tensions in the Middle East and arrange a second round of negotiations between the United States and Iran after almost seven weeks of war.

entries.title: Trump administration prepares for pivot to economic warfare on Iran
entries.snippet: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told reporters at a White House briefing Wednesday that the U.S. plans to ramp up economic pain on Iran, and said the new moves will be the "financial equivalent" of a bombing campaign.

entries.title: House Democrats attempt anti-corruption message to gain traction against Trump
entries.snippet: Days after Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán was ousted by an opposition campaign with an anti-corruption message, Democrats want to try the same playbook against President Donald Trump before the midterm elections.

entries.title: With U.S.-Iran ceasefire expiring in a week, diplomats lay groundwork for new talks
entries.snippet: International mediators are pushing for new peace talks between the United States and Iran, and they're seeking to extend the fragile ceasefire that's set to expire next week. But nothing is set in stone, and the sides remain far apart on issues like the fate of Iran's nuclear pr

entries.title: Israel's campaign in Lebanon a 'humanitarian catastrophe,' UN refugee chief warns
entries.snippet: The toll of the wider war in the Middle East has been severe in Lebanon. Israel has invaded the country's south again and is demanding that people vacate more land. The United Nations' top refugee official, Barham Salih, is there on a mission to survey the crisis and he sat down

entries.title: Sexual abuse allegations spur calls for a broader reckoning in Congress
entries.snippet: Resignations came quickly this week from two congressmen accused of sexual misconduct toward staff members. Yet for many of the women of Capitol Hill, the moment of accountability was years in the making — and far from enough.

entries.title: News Wrap: IRS says average tax refund just under $3,500 this year
entries.snippet: n our news wrap Wednesday, tax refunds are larger this year, but not nearly as big as the Trump administration had projected, the head of the IRS told a Senate hearing that his agency is working to address taxes owed but not paid and officials in Turkey say a student opened fire

entries.title: Live Nation and Ticketmaster abused monopoly power and gouged consumers, jury finds
entries.snippet: A jury found Live Nation and its subsidiary Ticketmaster are a monopoly in violation of federal and state laws. The trial could have implications for both the company and the entertainment industry. States argued Live Nation used its control of ticketing platforms and concert ven

entries.title: Trump's DOJ targets Jan. 6 convictions, broadens Fed construction site probe
entries.snippet: President Trump's Department of Justice is making headlines. This week, it is releasing a report accusing the Biden administration of weaponizing the agency, moving to wipe out the convictions of Jan. 6 ringleaders and making a surprise visit to a Federal Reserve construction sit

entries.title: Pope Leo's critiques of Trump policies hold more weight as a fellow American, priest says
entries.snippet: The battle between the president and the pope continues to play out. Pope Leo has not backed down from his criticisms of the war in Iran, but he is taking a different approach during his visit to Africa. Some allies of President Trump are also piling on to defend him and the war.

entries.title: Why the spirit of volunteering is a defining part of America's identity
entries.snippet: Before the United States was founded, the idea of volunteering was well underway in the colonies. Now, Americans consistently donate more of their time than citizens of any other country. Judy Woodruff explores how the idea took root and why it's become a part of our national ide

entries.title: New book explores Queen Elizabeth's relationships with 13 U.S. presidents
entries.snippet: Like countless powerful women throughout history, Queen Elizabeth II was routinely dismissed and underestimated. But during her 70-year reign, she managed the strategic relationship between the UK and the U.S., meeting with thirteen sitting presidents. Geoff Bennett spoke with Su

entries.title: What to know about new direct talks between Lebanon and Israel
entries.snippet: Lebanon and Israel's first direct talks in decades this week, spurred by the latest war between Israel and the Iran-backed Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, have been met by both high hopes and condemnation.

entries.title: Wall Street hits record as S&P 500 continues 2-week rally, boosted by hopes for Iran war's end
entries.snippet: The S&P 500 rose 0.8% and eclipsed its prior all-time high set in January. Whether Wall Street is correct to have so much hope for peace and whether stocks should be the highest they've ever been remains to be seen.

entries.title: Live Results: New Jersey special congressional election to replace Mikie Sherrill
entries.snippet: Residents of New Jersey's 11th Congressional District will have a representative in Congress for the first time this year after a special election on Thursday.

entries.title: WATCH: First lady Melania Trump advocates for U.S. foster care system improvements at House roundtable
entries.snippet: On Capitol Hill, Melania Trump said youngsters in foster care face barriers to housing, transportation and education and other challenges outside the classroom that affect their academic performance. "We can close this gap," she said.

entries.title: WATCH LIVE: OMB Director Vought testifies before Senate panel on Trump's 2027 budget request
entries.snippet: Russell Vought, a key figure in the Trump administration's efforts to shrink the federal workforce, will defend the president's budget request in congressional hearings again on Thursday.

entries.title: Ticketmaster and Live Nation had monopoly over big concert venues, jury finds
entries.snippet: A Manhattan federal jury deliberated for four days before reaching its decision Wednesday in the closely watched case, which gave fans the equivalent of a backstage pass to a business that dominates live entertainment in the U.S. and beyond.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Can Trump get a better Iran deal than Obama?
entries.snippet: The 2015 Iran deal curbed Tehran's nuclear program until the US pulled out. After years of failed diplomacy and 40 days of war, talks are back on. Donald Trump has said he can strike a "better" deal than Barack Obama.

entries.title: Germany, the UK warn the Iran war distracts from Ukraine and oil price rises help Russia
entries.snippet: Germany's defense minister warned that Russia "benefits from current developments in the Middle East," as the Ukraine Defense Contact Group met in Berlin. He said rising oil prices were filling Moscow's "war coffers."

entries.title: Berlin hosts Sudan Conference: a brutal, forgotten conflict
entries.snippet: The war in Sudan has been raging for the past three years, and a ceasefire is nowhere in sight. Participants at a conference of donors in Berlin hope to at least ease the suffering of the people.

entries.title: Germany: Sudan aid conference in Berlin raises €1.3B
entries.snippet: The war in Sudan is entering its fourth year with international attention focused on other conflicts. The conference in Berlin is aiming to bring back some attention to help fund urgent projects.

entries.title: South Africa appoints veteran Afrikaner politician Roelf Meyer as US ambassador amid tense Trump ties
entries.snippet: South Africa has appointed a prominent Afrikaner politician, Roelf Meyer, to the ambassadorial role in Washington. This comes amid allegations from the Trump administration of a "white genocide" in the country.

entries.title: German health minister announces billions in cutbacks
entries.snippet: The comprehensive reform package aims to plug a multibillion-euro hole in Germany's expensive health care system. Reforms include mandatory second opinions for costly surgeries and no more coverage for homeopathy.

entries.title: What's behind Israel's attacks along Lebanon's Litani River?
entries.snippet: Israel is pushing forward with plans to remove Lebanese homes and residents from between the Litani River and its own border, creating a buffer zone. Why is the area strategically important in the Middle East conflict?

entries.title: Iran: Hundreds of thousands of jobs lost due to war
entries.snippet: After six weeks of war, job losses are growing in Iran. Destroyed industrial facilities have brought production in many sectors to a standstill, hitting Iranian workers particularly hard.

entries.title: EU chief urges bloc-wide push on age verification app to protect children online
entries.snippet: EU chief calls for a bloc-wide push on an age verification app to protect children online. If enforced, users will have to prove their age to access legally restricted sites.

entries.title: Attacks on US academics: A microcosm of a larger threat to democracy
entries.snippet: In the new DW documentary "Democracy Under Attack: Can Dündar and Trump's America," the Turkish press freedom icon looks at the parallels between the erosion of democracy in the US and his home country.

entries.title: India's aviation boom hits turbulence amid Iran war
entries.snippet: From longer routes to higher fares, the Middle East conflict is exposing vulnerabilities in India's fast-growing airline sector.

entries.title: Turkey: School shooting leaves 9 dead
entries.snippet: The deadly shooting comes just a day after a similar incident in the country in which 16 people were injured.

entries.title: Iran war: White House mulling fresh talks with Tehran
entries.snippet: The Trump administration is considering plans for a second round of in-person talks between the US and Iran in Pakistan, White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said. DW has the latest.

entries.title: Solar power in Morocco's desert: Bold vision, mixed results
entries.snippet: A massive solar tower in the Moroccan desert is the beacon of an ambitious push for a clean energy future. But fossil fuels and grid constraints stand in the way.

entries.title: Hungary's Magyar hoping to form new government in early May
entries.snippet: Hungary's new government could take power in just a few weeks after Peter Magyar met with President Solyuk. Magyar also called on the Orban-aligned president to step down.

entries.title: Kanye West postpones France concert after UK visa denial
entries.snippet: Earlier in April, West was denied a UK visa, which led to the cancellation of his headline appearance at Wireless Festival. The entry denial was based on his repeated antisemitic remarks.

entries.title: Xi hails China-Russia ties, signals bigger role in Iran war
entries.snippet: Xi calls partnership with Russia "precious," as world leaders turn to China amid the Middle East crisis.

entries.title: Women's workwear: When proper fit becomes a safety issue
entries.snippet: With limited options, many women in trades and industry have to wear men's workwear. But a poor fit causes discomfort and can compromise safety. Now, some firms are stepping up to design safer, better-fitting gear.

entries.title: What we call sex work — and what it says about society
entries.snippet: A Bonn exhibition traces the cultural history of sex work and shows how the words used to describe it have shaped stigma, policy and lived experience.
